entry.title: Re: System is not allowing us to create one additional cu...
entry.content:
This is misunderstanding for a "Profile" equals a "Rule."

In reality, a single COGS Splitting Profile acts as a container. Within that one profile, you can define different behaviors for HU/RO (Manufacturing) and BG (Trading) by using the Source Accounts for Splitting.

1. How to Map the Requirement

  1. Maintain the Standard Profile: Use the pre-delivered profile (typically 0YA000).

  2. Differentiate by Valuation Variant: Since HU/RO are manufacturing entities, their COGS splitting is driven by the Cost Estimate (Production). Since BG is a trading entity, their COGS splitting for intercompany purchases can be driven by the Standard Price or Intercompany Billing components.

  3. Company Code Assignment: In the sub-folder "Company Codes Settings" you link HU, RO, and BG all to the same Profile ID. The system then uses the underlying Account/Cost Component mapping to decide how to split the values for each specific transaction.

2. Technical Solution for the "BG" Trading Scenario

For the BG country (which only sells finished goods from HU/RO or third parties), the "splitting" usually refers to breaking down the purchase price into cost components (e.g., Material, Freight, Intercompany Profit).

  • For HU/RO (Manufacturing): The split pulls from the released Product Cost Estimate.

  • For BG (Trading): When BG sells a product bought from HU, the system can still perform a COGS split if a Cost Estimate exists for that material in the BG plant (e.g., via Special Procurement Key 40 - Stock Transfer).

  • Refined Mapping: If BG uses a different set of G/L accounts for its COGS, you can map those specific accounts to different "Cost Component Groups" within the same profile.

entry.title: Manage the pending quantities to confirm in the Sales Order - RAP BO Events I_SALESORDERTP
entry.content:
This requirement is to be implemented in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system.

The requirement is to manage the pending quantities to be confirmed in the sales order. New items should be added to the sales order for unconfirmed quantities, and the existing item quantity should be updated according to the confirmed quantity.

The standard functionality in SAP Sales LoB allows users to use a specific item category to create a purchase requisition for a specific material. Therefore, after a new line item is added to the sales order, a purchase requisition can be created for that item category within the sales order.

Pre-requisites : 

  • Required Business Roles must be assigned to the user. 
  • Configuration for creation of purchase requisition for specific item category in Sales Order.

Solution: 

When a sales order is created in the S/4 system, the standard event “Sales Order Created” is raised with the following fields: EventRaisedDateTime, SalesOrder, SalesOrderType, SalesOrganization, DistributionChannel, OrganizationDivision, and SoldToParty.

The Sales Order Created event can be locally consumed within the same system in which it is raised. This local consumption is implemented with the help of an event handler class. The event handler class is set up as a class pool, similar to a behavior pool.

Refer SAP help documents used in this solution:

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/business-event-consumption

https://api.sap.com/bointerface/I_SALESORDERTP

Local Consumption ( DEV system Client 080 - ABAP Development Toolkit):

  1. To register a local consumption, custom ABAP Class is created and RAP BO i_salesordertp (name of behaviour definition of RAP BO) is specified.

    CLASS zcl_salesord_local_event_handl DEFINITION
    PUBLIC
    FINAL
    CREATE PUBLIC

    FOR EVENTS OF i_salesordertp.

    PUBLIC SECTION.
    PROTECTED SECTION.
    PRIVATE

  2.  Next, need to create a local handler class under Local Types of Global custom class which inherits from standard class CL_ABAP_BEHAVIOR_EVENT_HANDLER.

    CLASS lcl_so_event_cons DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.
    PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS:
    consume_create FOR ENTITY EVENT created_instances FOR salesorder~created.

    ENDCLASS.
    Note: SALESORDER~CREATED, which is stated after the FOR word.

    CREATED_INSTANCES is the name of importing payload which would have event data.
  3.  Released CDS view i_salesorderitem can be used to get additional data  - confddelivqtyinorderqtyunit, requestedquantity and other required fields. 
  4. For each instance o sales order items,  compare the REQUESTEDQUANTITY with CONFDDELIVQTYINORDERQTYUNIT and update Sales order as follows:
    • If both quantities are same – no update required.
    • If CONFDDELIVQTYINORDERQTYUNIT quantity is less then REQUESTEDQUANTITY then modify Entities I_SALESORDERTP and create new item by passing, quantity, product and category to CBAB.                                                                                                                                        MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_salesordertp
      ENTITY salesorder
      CREATE BY \_item
      * FROM lt_create "lt_sales_order_items
      FIELDS ( product
      requestedquantity salesorderitemcategory )
      WITH VALUE #( FOR ls_so_item1 IN lt_so_items
      (
      salesorder = ls_so_item1-salesorder
      %target = VALUE #( ( %cid = ls_so_item1-soitem
      product = ls_so_item1-product
      requestedquantity = ls_so_item1-so_quan
      salesorderitemcategory = ls_so_item1-item_cat ) ) ) )
      MAPPED DATA(ls_mapped)
      FAILED DATA(ls_failed)
      REPORTED DATA(ls_reported).
    • If there is no CONFDDELIVQTYINORDERQTYUNIT then update existing sales order item for category CBAB.
      MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_salesordertp ENTITY salesorderitem
      UPDATE
      FIELDS ( salesorderitemcategory requestedquantity )
      WITH VALUE #( FOR ls_so_upd1 IN lt_so_items_upd
      ( requestedquantity = ls_so_upd1-so_quan
      salesorderitemcategory = ls_so_upd1-item_cat
      %key-salesorder = ls_so_upd1-salesorder
      %key-salesorderitem = ls_so_upd1-soitem ) )
      FAILED DATA(ls_failed_upd)
      REPORTED DATA(ls_reported_upd).
  5. The above custom class will be executed after the sales order is committed in the S/4 system and will update the sales order as required. A locking issue may occur if the sales order is opened again in change mode before the consumption class execution is completed.                        

Troubleshooting:  Local Custom class created in ADT for event consumption can be debugged easily, and any issues encountered during support can be resolved more efficiently.

Conclusion:  In this use case, if we need to update application data within the system and no data needs to be retrieved from external sources, then this can be handled via local consumption of events and solution can be implemented using a RAP-based BO approach. There is no need to consume the event outside the system and then trigger the custom integration flow in the Integration Suite in BTP.

entry.title: Re: Revise the Business Partner Number Range Configuration

entry.title: Re: How to calculate bonus proration in Variable Pay usin...
entry.content: Thank you for the detailed explanation @Xavier. I think I should be able to work through the solution using the resources you've shared. By the way, this is my first Variable Pay implementation :). After calculating the final payout in a custom column once I obtain the exact dates required for the calculation. how to pass this calculated payout with Employee Central as a Non-Recurring Pay Component. In the Variable Pay – Employee Central Settings, I do not see any mapping available. The field “Field to control user publish” shows None, and when I open the dropdown, no values are displayed. However, in Salary Planning I used the <comp-ect-output-component> configuration as per SAP documents and it works perfectly fine. Could you please advise where I should map my custom calculated final payout (bonus) so that it publishes to the Non-Recurring Pay Component Bonus (1001) in Employee Central - Compensation?

entry.title: Re: Joule Navigational Scenario in SAP Work Zone Advanced
entry.content: Hi， There is a blog :https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/joule-is-now-integrated-into-sap-build-work-zone-advanced-edition/ba-p/14252774 about Joule in WZ advanced and how to start. And for prompt, you can refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/joule/capabilities-guide/joule-in-sap-build-work-zone-advanced-edition Best Regards, Aqua

entry.title: Re: Manage Product master data / work scheduling view / p...
entry.content: Hello @Chris1973, first of all, thank you for your reply. This corresponds to the procedure I carried out. However, I am having problems with the work scheduling view. I am missing the production scheduling profile. Furthermore, the customer does not necessarily have to maintain data in the work preparation view. In my experience, in this case, the work scheduling view is displayed but not created. As a result, no production orders can be created. What is the procedure if the customer does not need to maintain any fields from the view? Do they have to go to MM01 instead to create this view? Regards Florian

entry.title: Re: Prepare for refresh with /SDF/CALM_SYS_REFRESH
entry.content: Transport of Copies (ToC) are easy going transports which skip the strict rules of the CTS. They ignore transport routes and locks. If you do this often, you may also add a virtual system (example: DUM) to your transport domain and use this as a target. That is already enough to have the ToC exported.

entry.title: Re: Not able to see output - Adobe forms service & output...
entry.content: Its seems there is ADS issue, please contact your Basis team to have a look on the errror.

entry.title: Re: Using Concur API in Build Proces Automation
entry.content: @cherry_li thank you for your response. Unfortunately Concur doesn't show up in the list of Destinations like Integrated Product Development (IPD) in the example does, and I need support with correctly setting up this destination.

entry.title: Transformation Fatigue: The Hidden Risk in Continuous Business Change
entry.content:
What Is Transformation Fatigue?

Transformation fatigue is the psychological and emotional exhaustion that employees experience when subjected to repeated, overlapping, or poorly managed change initiatives. It manifests not as open rebellion but as quiet disengagement — the meeting attendance that becomes performative, the survey scores that plateau, the tribal knowledge that quietly walks out the door.

It is distinct from change resistance. Resistance is active and often signals disagreement with the direction. Fatigue is passive — employees have simply run out of cognitive and emotional bandwidth to engage authentically.

McKinsey research consistently shows that approximately 70% of transformation programs fail to achieve their stated goals. While much of the narrative focuses on strategy and execution failures, the human dimension — the exhaustion of the people being asked to transform — is chronically underweighted.

The Root Causes: Why Employees Hit the Wall

  1. Initiative Stacking Without Completion

Most large organizations are running three to seven major transformation programs simultaneously. ERP upgrades sit alongside digital experience redesigns, which run parallel to workforce restructuring, which overlaps with a cloud migration. Each initiative arrives with its own governance structure, steering committee, and 'transformation office.'

Employees are asked to maintain business as usual while absorbing change across multiple dimensions at once. There is no recovery period — no organizational breathing space. The result is that each new initiative lands on already depleted bandwidth.

  1. The Promise-Reality Gap

Transformation fatigue accelerates when the lived experience of employees diverges dramatically from the narrative leadership communicates. When a major ERP rollout is described as 'empowering people with better tools' but delivers six months of degraded productivity, increased manual workarounds, and two rounds of remedial training — the credibility of future transformation promises is eroded.

This gap compounds over time. By the third or fourth major initiative, employees have developed a sophisticated internal algorithm: discount the headline promise, brace for disruption, wait to see what survives 18 months.

  1. Loss of Perceived Agency

Sustained transformation without genuine participation destroys the sense of agency that motivates high performance. When employees feel that change is happening to them rather than with them, engagement collapses. This is particularly acute in middle management layers — the cohort asked to implement change they had no hand in designing, while managing upward anxiety and downward morale simultaneously.

  1. Grief That Goes Unacknowledged

Every transformation involves loss — of familiar processes, team structures, career pathways, or organizational identity. When leadership focuses entirely on the destination and fails to acknowledge what is being left behind, employees grieve without permission. Unprocessed organizational grief becomes a weight that makes forward movement feel impossible.

 

"Every transformation involves loss. The organizations that acknowledge this — rather than rushing past it — build the psychological safety that makes real change possible."

 

Case Illustrations: Fatigue in the Real World

ERP Transformations: The Decade-Long Marathon

Enterprise resource planning implementations are among the most demanding organizational changes a business can undertake. Multi-year timelines, cross-functional disruption, and the brutal middle period of parallel running — where teams essentially do every job twice — create conditions for profound fatigue.

A typical pattern: Year one brings genuine energy and executive sponsorship. Year two brings scope creep, delayed timelines, and the departure of the change champion. Year three brings stabilization fatigue — the exhaustion of people who have been 'in transformation' so long they can no longer distinguish normal operations from the project. When post-go-live optimizations are then announced as a 'Phase 2 transformation,' the eye-rolls are almost audible.

The fatigue here is particularly destructive because ERP implementations require sustained behavioral change, not just technical adoption. Tired users revert to shadow spreadsheets. Workarounds proliferate. The platform that cost tens of millions to implement is used at 40% of its designed capability.

Digital Transformation: The Perpetual Horizon

'Digital transformation' has become perhaps the most durable source of transformation fatigue in modern business — in part because it never ends. Unlike a discrete project with a clear completion date, digital transformation is positioned as an ongoing state of becoming. This framing, while intellectually honest, is psychologically exhausting.

Technology teams in large enterprises frequently report cycling through platform migrations — from on-premise to hybrid cloud, from hybrid cloud to multi-cloud, from legacy CRM to Salesforce, from Salesforce to bespoke AI-powered platforms — before any given solution reaches maturity. Employees who invested months learning a system watch it be sunset before they ever felt genuinely competent. The result is learned helplessness: why invest in mastery when mastery will be rendered obsolete?

Restructuring: The Wound That Reopens

Organizational restructuring produces some of the sharpest fatigue because it strikes at identity and security simultaneously. When a company announces its third restructure in five years — each time with the language of 'right-sizing for the future' — employees who survived the previous rounds carry what organizational psychologists call 'survivor guilt' alongside hypervigilance about their own vulnerability.

This chronic uncertainty impairs precisely the strategic thinking, creative risk-taking, and collaborative investment that transformations require. People in survival mode do not transform. They endure.

 

How Leadership Communication Either Fuels or Fights Fatigue

Leadership communication is the single most leveraged variable in transformation fatigue. The same change program, with identical resourcing and strategy, will produce fundamentally different outcomes depending on how leaders communicate.

What Makes Communication Dangerous

  • Cascading optimism that erases difficulty — 'this will be seamless' — destroys credibility when difficulty inevitably arrives
  • Frequency without substance — weekly updates that repeat the same milestones without acknowledging reality exhaust rather than reassure
  • Invisible leaders — senior sponsors who announce transformation then disappear from visible engagement send a signal that the initiative is performative
  • The 'burning platform' overused — invoking existential threat to drive urgency works once; used repeatedly, it induces anxiety without motivation

 

What Makes Communication Restorative

  • Honest acknowledgement of difficulty: 'This will be hard, and here is how we will support you through it'
  • Visible leaders doing the work — executives who use the new ERP, who attend the same training as their teams, who visibly model the change
  • Closure and celebration of what came before — honoring the previous state before moving on legitimizes the loss and enables forward momentum
  • Distinguishing signal from noise — giving employees explicit permission to stop engaging with abandoned initiatives is one of the most underrated acts of leadership generosity
  • Honest status communication — sharing what is not working, and why, dramatically increases trust and reduces the cynical narrative that always fills information vacuums

 

"The antidote to transformation fatigue is not less change — it is change that is designed with the human experience at its center, not as an afterthought."

 

A Framework for Sustainable Transformation

The following framework, the PACE model, offers leaders a structured approach to designing transformations that build organizational capacity rather than deplete it. Each phase addresses a different dimension of the fatigue problem.

 

PHASE

KEY LEADER ACTIONS

OUTCOME

P — Prioritize & Sequence

Audit the full transformation portfolio. Ruthlessly sequence initiatives. Establish a formal 'initiative capacity' metric before launching new programs.

Reduced cognitive overload; clear organizational focus

A — Acknowledge & Grieve

Formally close previous initiatives. Celebrate what was built before. Create structured space for teams to process what is being lost in each transition.

Psychological safety; trust in leadership honesty

C — Co-create & Commit

Involve the people most affected in designing the change. Give middle managers genuine design authority, not consultation theatre. Publish commitments publicly.

Employee agency; authentic buy-in beyond compliance

E — Embed & Exhale

Build explicit consolidation periods into program timelines. Define 'done' clearly. Resist the temptation to layer the next initiative before stabilization is complete.

Organizational resilience; restored capacity for next change

Applying the PACE Model: What It Looks Like in Practice

Applying PACE to an ERP context might mean: delaying the cloud migration announcement until the ERP has been live and stable for 12 months (Prioritize & Sequence); hosting structured 'legacy system retirement' sessions where teams formally honor the old process before deleting it (Acknowledge & Grieve); establishing working groups of power users who co-design the Phase 2 feature roadmap (Co-create & Commit); and then setting a hard 18-month moratorium on new platform changes post go-live (Embed & Exhale).

It sounds simple. It is, in fact, radical — because it requires leaders to voluntarily slow the pace of their own ambition in service of organizational health.

The Leadership Imperative

Transformation fatigue is not a soft problem. It is a business risk. It degrades the return on transformation investment, accelerates talent attrition among the most capable people — who have the most options — and creates brittle organizations that cannot respond adaptively to genuine disruption.

The leaders who will build enduring organizations in the decade ahead are not those who drive the most change. They are those who understand that organizational change capacity is a finite, renewable resource — and who steward it with the same discipline they bring to financial capital.

Slow down to speed up. Acknowledge the cost. Design the human experience, not just the process. These are not soft leadership platitudes. In a world of perpetual disruption, they are the hardest-edged competitive advantages available.

 

"The leaders who will build enduring organizations are not those who drive the most change — they are those who understand that change capacity is a finite, renewable resource to be stewarded with discipline."

 

Found this valuable?

Share with a colleague navigating a transformation, or comment below with your experience. What has your organization done to manage transformation fatigue effectively? The best answers might shape a follow-up article.

entry.title: Re: How to add style and standard paragraph for Footer te...
entry.content:
For the style, as far as I know, it needs to be maintained directly in the Adobe Forms.

For the footer text, you can add it using the Manage Text app.

entry.title: Re: How to calculate bonus proration in Variable Pay using a lookup table?
entry.content: Thank you for the detailed explanation. I believe I should be able to work through the solution using the resources you've shared. By the way, this is my first Variable Pay implementation 🙂. My plan is to calculate the final payout in a custom column once I obtain the exact dates required for the calculation. My question is how to pass this calculated payout to Employee Central as a Non-Recurring Pay Component. In Variable Pay – Employee Central Settings, I do not see any mapping available. The field “Field to control user publish” shows None, and when I open the dropdown, no values are displayed. However, in Salary Planning I used the <comp-ect-output-component> configuration as described in the SAP documentation, and it works perfectly fine. Could you please advise where I should map my custom calculated final payout (bonus) so that it publishes to the Non-Recurring Pay Component Bonus (1001) in Employee Central?

entry.title: Re: Issue with ANS HTML mail Template
entry.content:
Hello Asif, 

there was a bug related to that functionality that was fixed and just recently went in Production.

This should now work for you. Let me know if you still have issues. 

Regards,

Stoil

entry.title: New AI feature arrived - From Idea to Event-Driven Architecture with SAP Advanced Event Mesh
entry.content:
Event-driven architectures (EDA) are increasingly used in modern integration landscapes to enable loosely coupled, scalable, and reactive systems. Within the SAP ecosystem, events allow business applications to notify other services about state changes without requiring synchronous APIs or direct dependencies.

However, implementing an event-driven architecture involves more than simply provisioning messaging infrastructure. Architects must translate business scenarios into events, topics, and subscriptions, and ensure that producers and consumers interact through well-defined contracts.

Those setup activities can be done manually but now also automated  & AI-assisted.

 

From Business Description to Event Architecture

The new feature in SAP Advanced Event Mesh helps accelerate the design process by generating an initial event architecture model from a simple description of a business scenario.

For example, an architect might describe a retail company with an order management system, a commerce platform, and analytics services. Based on this description, the system can generate a proposed event architecture including:

  • application domains

  • event producers and consumers

  • event definitions

  • topic hierarchies

  • event schemas

This generated architecture provides a starting point for architects, who can then refine and adjust the design to match their organization's requirements.

Instead of manually defining each element, teams can begin with a structured model and iterate from there.

Component Role
S/4HANA systemPublishes order events and finance actions
SAP EWM systemWarehouse tasks
SAP TMTracks transport related actions
SAP BDCConsumes all events

In the SAP ecosystem, systems such as SAP S/4HANA can emit business events whenever key business objects change. These events can then be distributed through SAP Advanced Event Mesh to interested consumers.

  • Event domains (e.g., Warehouse Order)

  • Event types (OrderCreated, OrderUpdated)

  • Event producers

  • Event consumers

This forms the foundation for the event model and the AI agant will suggest the required Applications and Events.

Config Push the Event Flow

Once the event architecture has been defined, the final step is pushing the design time objects to the runtime infrastructure (broker).

You can check the created objects in the Catalog /Design Time

 

 

Conclusion

Designing event-driven architectures traditionally requires careful planning of domains, events, and topic structures before any runtime infrastructure is deployed.

With the latest capability in SAP Advanced Event Mesh, architects can accelerate this process by generating an initial event architecture from a business description and refining it into a production-ready design.

Combined with the runtime capabilities of the event mesh, this approach allows organizations to move faster from idea to implementation, enabling scalable event-driven integration across their SAP landscapes.

entry.title: Re: ASE 16.1 didn't start as a windows service
entry.content: 3631971 - SAP ASE Windows service fails to start with error 1067 when using served license

entry.title: Re: Build Work Zone Returning 401
entry.content: Hi, please raise a support ticket under a matched component as described in the document here https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-standard-edition/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition/troubleshooting-e69b6f5d31a542c6a5f87c8b45c1d427?q=component

entry.title: Re: My purchase requisition partial cancellation confirma...
entry.content:
Dear @arnaudf,

Because the issue occurs only in French and the same scenario works in English, this strongly suggests a language-dependent unit-of-measure conversion issue, not a business-process limitation. The French popup indicates a conversion problem between units of measure. SAP documents that unit-of-measure validation is language dependent and that the system links external/commercial units and internal units based on the user’s logon language. SAP also documents that missing language-specific UoM maintenance can cause exactly this type of issue. The first recommended check is therefore SSCUI 102179 Units of Measurement. SAP explicitly states that the unit of measure must exist in the language where the error occurs, and that missing translations in the logon language must be maintained there. In this case, the French language entries for the relevant UoM should be verified carefully

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3269863

In parallel, it is important to note that the old My Purchase Requisitions app is deprecated in Public Cloud and SAP recommends switching to My Purchase Requisitions New / Confirm Receipt of Goods New as the successor apps

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/713eae2544204816b71a88caa89bd256.html

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F4489')/S36

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1639A')/S35

Summary:

  1. Verify the French UoM maintenance in SSCUI 102179 Units of Measurement
  2. Retest the same scenario in My Purchase Requisitions New or Confirm Receipt of Goods New because the old app is deprecated
  3. If the UoM entries are correct and the issue still occurs only in French, raise an SAP incident

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Enterprise AI in Action: SAP Joule Delivers Where Others Don’t
entry.content: Thank you Manisha for sharing this insightful comparison, it will indeed help to explain to customer better.

entry.title: Value Help Restrictions in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Do They Filter the F4 Help?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently analysing the behaviour of Value Help restrictions in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and would like to clarify whether my understanding of the concept is correct.

My question is not limited to a specific field, but concerns the general design of Value Help restrictions in business roles.

Background

In the Maintain Business Roles app it is possible to define Value Help restrictions for certain fields within an access category.

The expectation from a usability and security perspective would be that these restrictions limit the values displayed in the F4 value help, so that users only see entries they are allowed to use.

However, in my testing the behaviour appears different.

Example Scenario (Sales Order)

To illustrate the behaviour, I tested this in the sales order creation process.

Applications tested:

  • VA01 – Create Sales Order

  • F3893 –  (Fiori)

Goal:

Restrict the Sales Organization (VKORG) so that users can only work with a limited set of values.

Configuration steps:

  1. Open Maintain Business Roles.

  2. Edit the business role.

  3. Navigate to Restrictions.

  4. In the Sales Area section, maintain Value Help restrictions for Sales Organization.

  5. Example configuration:

    • Allow only a subset of Sales Organizations (e.g. 1710, 1720)

    • Alternatively test with no entries maintained.

Observed Behaviour

When the user creates a sales order and presses F4 on the Sales Organization field:

  • The value help always shows all Sales Organizations in the system.

  • The maintained value help restrictions are not reflected in the F4 help.

This happens in both:

  • VA01 (GUI)

  • Fiori app F3893

However, when the user selects a Sales Organization that should not be allowed and tries to continue or save the sales order, the system raises an authorization error, for example:

“You are not authorized for Sales Organization XYZ”

This indicates that the restriction is technically evaluated, but only during document processing, not during the value help selection.

 

Conceptual Question

My main question is about the general design of Value Help restrictions in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Should Value Help restrictions:

Option A

Filter the F4 value help, so that users only see values they are authorized for.

or

Option B

Allow users to see all values in the value help, but enforce the restriction only when the document is processed or saved.

Additional Question

Independently of the Sales Organization example:

Is there any mechanism in S/4HANA Public Cloud that allows system-wide hiding of values in value help based on authorizations or restrictions?

In other words:

Is it generally possible that unauthorized values are not visible at all in F4 helps, or is the standard design that they remain visible and are only blocked during processing?

Any insights from others working with authorizations and business roles in S/4HANA Public Cloud would be very helpful.

Thank you.

David

entry.title: selling of Multiple Assets

entry.title: Re: The Future Has Been Written – the Rise of the Database Administrator
entry.content:
C_DBADM can now be scheduled as in the new format:
https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-database-administrator-sap-hana

entry.title: Re: Revise the Business Partner Number Range Configuration
entry.content:
Hello @Chanaporn

Yes, if you are referring to the BP configuration activity shown in your screenshot, then this is included in the same behaviour. It is the standard place to define number ranges for business partners in Public Cloud.

In other words, if your screenshot is the exact activity you are using, then the expected interpretation is still:

  • Customizing/Quality can have one interval
  • Production can keep a different manually maintained interval
  • The later transport does not reset Production automatically to the source values.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Accept/Revoke MRP Run results in the Stock/Requirements List and MRP Fiori Apps
entry.content:
This is a good solution but..

Beware, there is no security around who can press those accept/revoke buttons. If you have access to MD04 you have all the authorization you need.  For my organisation that is not a good solution as many people can look at MD04 but only a chosen few should be able to accept the MRP result.

Secondly while the name of the person who accepted the result is saved to table PPMRP_ACKNWLEDGE, it is not visible to an end user as a field in any screen.  We could accept that there was no security on the button, if the person's name who accepted the result was visible in any SAPGUI screen.

These concerns are from an organization practically trying to use this function.  If i have any of the above incorrect please someone correct me.

entry.title: Request Detailed Information for Pre-delivered Posting Rules
entry.content:
Dear Team,

How to get detailed information for pre-delivered posting rules?

Thank you,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Request Detailed Information for Pre-delivered Postin...
entry.content:
Hello,

Please refer to KBA 2854415 - Information on Bank Statement Posting Rules in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system - SAP for Me.

You can download all the posting rules from our help portal Posting Rules.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: France : electronic invoice routing address
entry.content:
Hello,

We are currently implementing electronic invoicing for French customers. Our client has opted for an external PDP (Plateforme de Dématérialisation Partenaire) and does not have a subscription to SAP DRC (Document and Reporting Compliance).

We are looking for a clean and sustainable solution to maintain the electronic invoice routing address (routing code/SIREN_SUFFIX,SIRET_SIRET,ect...) within the Customer Master Data.

Could you provide the current best practices or recommendations for customers running under SAP ECC or S/4HANA on-premises in this specific context?

Thank you in advance.

Hello Team,

 

we are looking forward to sell multiple assets from a company .

 

we couldnot find any possibile solution in sap ..?

 

Regards,

zareena

entry.title: Re: Getting Started with Joule Studio in SAP Build - Part 1: Provisioning Joule Studio
entry.content:
@Diganta_Mandal please add that the Joule foundation plan is sufficient from customer perspective.

The screenshots depict an internal SAP environment.

entry.title: SAP EAM - multiple checklists per maintenance order operation
entry.content:
Greetings,

we are wondering if it is possible to implement multiple checklist per maintenance order operation. We are aware of the possibility of having multiple checklists per maintenance order - one per operation.

We have tried the following:

  • adding multiple operating in a single inspection plan - in this example characteristics from all operations get joined in one checklist
  • classifying multiple inspection plans with the same checklist type value - the system created a checklists for the last valid inspection plan and ignores the others

We have a business scenario where we have a long checklist and we would like to structure it as best as possible to reduce administrative overhead. If we use "Perform maintenance jobs" to record results we would like to enter into all checklists from as few operations as possible - preferably 1.

Unfortunately due to the long list of characteristics we cannot group them all in one checklist because the list is just too long and not user friendly with a big probability for errors.

Any help would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Updating sales order user status via Patch operation
entry.content:
Hello, 

we are trying to update Sales order User status on header level in the context of Sales order CRUD API - API_SALES_ORDER_SRV through Patch operation. However, we are not able to do so. Tried to update through Property "StatusCode", but unfortunately the response is giving Property invalid even though this Property can be found in the metadata of the API. We are trying with the following batch request:

--batch
Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=changeset

--changeset
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary
Content-ID: 1

PATCH A_SalesOrder(SalesOrder='XXXXXXXXXX') HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
If-Match: *
Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8

{"StatusCode":"E0029"}


--changeset--
--batch--

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: 会计年度变式与年度相关去掉勾选
entry.content:
From blog In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA – Ledger Scoping Q&A - SAP Community

See section on  fiscal year and posting period variants:
See  Fiscal Year and Fiscal Year Variant

See also 3530183 - Assigning Accounting Principles to Ledgers and Company Codes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Details on Configuration in General Ledger Accounting and Asset Accounting - SAP for Me

entry.title: How to change the VAT number in the Billing document.
entry.content:
When a VAT registration number is changed in the Business Partner master data effective from a specific date (for example, 01.01.DIGITS), the system does not maintain time-dependency for this field.

As a result, when creating correction billing documents (such as credit memos or debit memos) with a billing date prior to the effective date of the VAT change, the VAT Registration Number cannot be modified in the billing document or on the printout.

Although the VAT number can be manually adjusted in the VAT control report, this leads to a discrepancy between the report and the printed billing document.

Question:

Is there a recommended approach or workaround to ensure that the correct VAT Registration Number is reflected in correction billing documents and their printouts when the VAT number has changed but historical documents need to reflect the previous value?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to change the VAT number in the Billing document.
entry.content:
The data from the referenced document is always copied, never re-determined. As a workaround it is possible to change the EU VAT ID in the billing document header via data transfer custom logic, field is billing_doc_res-VATRegistration .

  • Refer this for detailed information Custom Data Transfer for Billing Process Documents:

As a configuration expert and developer, you can implement the Custom Data Transfer for Billing Process Documents BAdI to control and automate specific aspects of this data transfer between documents. For example, in your case you can selectively overwrite specific fields with new values.

Implementing the BAdI:

  • You, as a developer, can set up the required custom logic by creating one or more implementations of the filter-enabled Business Add-In (BAdI) Custom Data Transfer for Billing Process Documents (SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER). You can do this in the Custom Logic app.
  • Changing Parameters BILL_DOC_RES ,  this structure contains a subset of the header fields imported via the BIL_DOC structure. You can pass new values to these fields using custom logic. 

Configuration Procedure

  1. Create an implementation of the BAdI Custom Data Transfer for Billing Process Documents (SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER) in Custom logic app.
  2. Perform the configuration activity Define Custom Routines for Data Transfer to Billing Documents.
  3. Depending on the types of reference documents for which you want to modify the data transfer routine, perform one or more of the following configuration activities.
  4. Define Copying Control for Sales Document to Billing Document (for example, for sales order to invoice)
  5. Define Copying Control for Delivery Document to Billing Document (for example, for outbound delivery to invoice)
  6. Define Copying Control for Billing Document to Billing Document (for example, for invoice to credit memo or billing document request to invoice)

     4.From this point on, when a billing clerk creates a billing process document with the relevant billing type, reference SD document type, and item category, the system calls the associated BAdI implementation and applies the implemented custom logic to the new billing process document.

entry.title: Creating Down Payment Request for Asset Using "Verify Down Payment Request" App
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

For processing a down payment request related to asset procurement, the request needs to be generated through the "Verify Down Payment Request" app in SAP.

To assist with this process, I would like to request a guideline outlining the required steps to create a down payment request for an asset using this application. A step-by-step instruction would be highly appreciated so that we can follow the correct procedure in the system.

entry.title: Discover What's New: SAP Fieldglass May 2026 Release Webinar
entry.content:
We’re pleased to invite you to the upcoming Discover What’s New in the SAP Fieldglass May 2026 Release webinar.

Join us to explore the exciting new features in the May 2026 SAP Fieldglass Release. This session will walk through the latest updates designed to make external workforce management more intuitive, efficient, and aligned with evolving business needs.

Webinar Details

  • Title: Discover What's New in the May 2026 Release webinar
  • Date: Tuesday, May 19th, 2026
  • Time: 11am EDT/4pm BST
  • Duration: 1 hour

How to Register

Click the link below to secure your spot:

Register Now

After registering, you’ll receive an email confirmation with calendar options.

If you’re unable to attend live, a recording will be shared with all registrants after the event.

entry.title: SLT Replication Error: Syntax error in runtime object – Missing IUUC_OPERAT_FLAG for CDS View
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am encountering an issue while trying to set up a replication for a CDS View in SAP Landscape Transformation (SLT). The generation of runtime objects fails with a syntax error in the generated function module.

Environment details:

Source: S/4HANA (relicating CDS views)

Migration Object: Z_TEST

Mass Transfer ID: 01H

The Error:
When I start the generation, the log shows the following messages:

Error while processing runtime objects for Z_ZTEST_01H

Syntax error in function module /1LT/SLQOLC_001000000175841

Message: The data object "_WA_R_ZTEST" does not have a component called "IUUC_OPERAT_FLAG".

Has anyone encountered this specific issue when replicating CDS views? Any help or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Guideline for Creating Down Payment Request for Asset
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

For processing a down payment request related to asset procurement, the request needs to be generated through the "Verify Down Payment Request" app in SAP.

To assist with this process, I would like to request a guideline outlining the required steps to create a down payment request for an asset using this application. A step-by-step instruction would be highly appreciated so that we can follow the correct procedure in the system.

entry.title: Individual Link Segmentation Not Working in Automation Program
entry.content:
We are currently trying to implement an Automation program requested.
However, it seems that the segmentation based on Individual Link under Email Behavior Segmentation is not functioning as expected, so we would like to ask for your advice.
 
<Automation Requirement>
(Please refer to images below)
1. Send <Email A> to the segmented contacts.
2. Branch Point 1: Contacts who did not open Email A will exit the program. 
3. Branch Point 2: Contacts who opened Email A and clicked a specific link will receive <Email B>. Contacts who opened Email A but did not click the specific link will receive <Email C>.

We have tried to set the Automation 1 & 2 as shown below.

Automation 1


Automation 2 

 

However, the results were not as expected.
Regardless of whether contacts opened the email and clicked the link or only opened the email, they all end up receiving the email on the same path.

Could you please review where are the incorrect points of  the Automation below?
If there are any parts that should be adjusted, we would greatly appreciate your feedback.
 
(In addition to the setup shown in the diagram, we have also tried several different approaches.
For example, we tried adding a Wait node before the second Filter Switch, and we also tested using positive conditions instead of negative conditions (e.g., Clicked Individual Link).
Also we tried seperating the program as well to test the 'Transfer to Program' node.
But they all failed and have same issue as I wrote above.)

entry.title: Re: How to Count Table Entries with a Custom KPI
entry.content:
Hello @@stephaniejerch

 
Thankyou for detailed blog. however we do have 100's of system to manage and above said coding has to be done in all systems(and tables)
 
While usage of the scneario is easy in SOLMAN, the above process looks complicated using in SAP CLOUD ALM. Besides for any custom code being done has to be managed during upgrades which is making complex.
 
Is there any plan in future for SAP to make table entry counter monitoring more easy. Reason is currently we have around 1400 Monitoring objects for Table entry Monitoring which means 1400 sub methods has to be defined.

entry.title: AI in warehousing -what you need to start with Joule
entry.content:
Today, I want to focus on a topic that is transforming user interaction across our solutions: Joule, your AI copilot. Many of you are eager to integrate this powerful assistant into your SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment, and a common question we receive is, "How do we get started?"

The idea came when we have presented the webinar “What´s New: SAP Extended Warehouse Management 2025 FPS1 Update” and Corrine Lu presented, what the new AI capabilities are for SAP EWM.
Joule has begun supporting transactional actions in EWM, starting with outbound processes. Users can now ask Joule to:

  • Create or confirm warehouse tasks
  • Set shipping readiness status (integrated with TM)
  • Post or reverse goods issues
    This is part of an ongoing rollout, with new Joule skills being released monthly.


Joule integration with the SAP EWM Outbound process

In the upcoming releases of Joule monthly schedule, we will release the full capabilities of Joule feature “Talk to your Warehouse Outbound process”, providing users with the possibility not only to retrieve data from EWM backend on Outbound Delivery Orders, but also to trigger next steps and accelerate the outbound fulfillment process. In addition, users can also navigate directly in the Joule conversation to the corresponding FIORI apps.

Get an overview on all available Joule capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition via our dedicated SAP Help – Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - highlighting transactional and informational capabilities to stay up-to-date.

Unlocking Joule: The Onboarding Pathway

Integrating Joule into your landscape is a structured process designed to connect the AI copilot securely and efficiently with your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. Based on the official onboarding guide, the journey can be understood through its prerequisites and a clear, multi-step setup.

Before You Begin: Essential Prerequisites

To ensure a smooth onboarding experience, it is essential to have the following in place first:

* An SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) global account.
* An SAP BTP subaccount provisioned in a supported region (for example, US East (VA) or Europe (Frankfurt)).
* The Cloud Foundry environment must be enabled within your subaccount.
* Necessary entitlements for both Joule and SAP Build Process Automation.
* Appropriate Administrator roles in both your BTP global and subaccounts.

Meeting these requirements beforehand will pave the way for a successful integration.


Often asked: the licenses that you need to start with Joule

Before diving into the Joule onboarding tasks, it is essential to first ensure that certain necessary prerequisites are met, both for the SAP product and for Joule itself. Let’s start by examining the SAP product prerequisites that need to be in place.

  • Valid SAP Product License: The organization must hold a license for an SAP product that supports Joule integration (such as SAP SuccessFactors or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition) hosted in one of the supported data centers. 
  • Identity Authentication Integration: The SAP product must be integrated with Identity Authentication Service (IAS). Joule leverages the existing IAS setup of the SAP product to handle user login and authentication.
  • SAP BTP Enterprise Global Account: An enterprise global account on SAP Business Technology Platform is required. Since Joule is a BTP-based application, it relies on SAP BTP services to function properly.

 

For a smooth onboarding experience, users must ensure that the following prerequisites for Joule are in place:

  • Cloud Identity Services Entitlements: The organization must have the necessary entitlements for Cloud Identity Services. Joule utilizes Identity Authentication for user login, while the Identity Provisioning service handles the provisioning of identities and their authorizations across various business applications, including SAP SuccessFactors, the Identity Authentication Service Tenant, and SAP Build Work Zone.
  • Dedicated Subaccount Creation: A subaccount must be created within the global account using the SAP BTP cockpit for provisioning Joule. While a single Joule subscription can be integrated with multiple SAP products (such as SAP SuccessFactors, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP Build Work Zone), it’s important to note that one subscription can only be integrated with one tenant of any given SAP product. This means multiple systems of the same product cannot be included within a single Joule formation. Organizations must ensure there is one IAS tenant per independent entity and operate each independent entity from its own dedicated SAP BTP subaccount.

For more details regarding the Joule specific requirements, as well as which SAP product prerequisites are needed, please read the Help Portal Page “Integrating Joule with SAP Solutions: Prerequisites”

 Your Onboarding Journey: A Step-by-Step Overview

The core onboarding process involves configuring your BTP subaccount to communicate with Joule and your target application. The official guide breaks this down into four primary stages:

1. Subscribe to Joule: The first step is to subscribe to the Joule application within your designated SAP BTP subaccount. This formally provisions the service for your use.
2. Set Up Roles and Trust: Security and proper governance are paramount. This stage involves establishing the necessary trust configuration and assigning roles to ensure that only authorized users can interact with Joule.
3. Configure Destinations: Next, you will set up a destination for SAP Build Process Automation. This is a crucial step that facilitates communication and enables Joule to execute tasks.
4. Integrate with Your Application: The final stage involves integrating Joule with your target system, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud. This makes the Joule interface available to your end-users directly within their work environment.

Once these steps are completed, Joule will be active and ready to assist your users.

What Can You Expect from Joule?

With Joule successfully onboarded, your organization can begin leveraging its powerful capabilities, which are continuously expanding. As we've highlighted in our roadmap discussions, Joule is designed to assist with:

* Informational Skills: Users can ask natural language questions about business objects, system configurations, and more, with Joule drawing information directly from the SAP Help Portal.
* Navigational Skills: Joule can guide users directly to the Fiori apps and transactions they need to perform their tasks, simplifying system navigation.
* Transactional Skills: This is an evolving area where we are making significant investments. Users can instruct Joule to perform actions, such as creating or confirming tasks, accelerating processes for urgent orders.

We at SAP are committed to delivering a harmonized and intelligent user experience. The onboarding of Joule is your next step into this exciting future. For the most detailed and up-to-date instructions, I encourage you to consult the official Onboarding Joule documentation

entry.title: Re: Mastering Data Loads in SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 - Proven Strategies for Accuracy &&
entry.content:
Thank you for this very insightful blog, Satya.
It provides a great overview of what to consider and highlights the challenges that may arise depending on whether a manual or an API-based migration approach is chosen.
From my experience, I would also like to add that the time required for business configuration in SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2, as well as in the backend system, should not be underestimated. It is also important to consider any customer-specific extensions that may need to be implemented on SAP BTP.
For customers planning to migrate from SAP Sales Cloud V1 to V2 using the Data Transfer Tool, I can also recommend the following SAP blog.
https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blog-posts-by-sap/your-path-to-sap-sales-amp-service-cloud-v2-starts-here-understanding-the/ba-p/14283154

entry.title: Re: Week 1 : Set up the Basic Trial account enabled with Joule for Developers
entry.content: ZAC_1405660

entry.title: Portugal’s New Deposit & Refund System (SDR): What Businesses and Consumers Need to Know
entry.content:
Legal Background: Extended Producer Responsibility

The SDR is part of Portugal’s broader waste‑management framework aligned with the principle of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR), already established in national law for packaging and packaging waste. Under this principle, companies that place packaged beverages on the market are responsible for ensuring that the packaging is properly identified to allow the collection and recycling the end of its life.

What Is the SDR and How Does It Work?

The Deposit and Refund System apply to single‑use beverage containers made of plastic (PET), metal, or aluminum, up to 3 liters. Consumers pay a €0.10 deposit when purchasing a qualifying beverage. This deposit is refunded once the empty container is returned to an authorized collection point.

How Consumers Get Their Refund

  • Return points include supermarkets, retail partners, manual collection counters, and approximately 2,500 automated return machines installed nationwide.
  • Containers must be intact, empty, have a readable barcode, and display the official Volta SDR symbol.
  • The refund is issued as:
    • cash,
    • store credit,
    • digital credit,
    • or a donation to charity.

Why “VOLTA”? The New National SDR Brand

The government and SDR Portugal launched VOLTA, the official national identity for the system. Volta aims not only to unify communication but also to connect citizens emotionally to the idea that “a bottle becomes a bottle again.”

During a transition period, some non‑Volta‑marked containers may still circulate, but by August 2026 the deposit system becomes mandatory.  

The Flow of Plastic Bottles Under SDR

Here is the simplified path a plastic bottle follows under the new regime:

  • Production or Import

Producers and importers placing beverages on the Portuguese market must register with the national producer registry and comply with EPR obligations.

  • Deposit Payment at Purchase

Consumers pay the €0.10 deposit added to the product price.

  • Consumption and Return

After consuming the beverage, the consumer returns the container to an SDR return point to receive the refund.

  • Collection and Sorting

Returned containers are sent to SDR sorting centers where they are counted, separated by material (PET, aluminum, etc.), compacted and prepared for recycling.

What Businesses Must Prepare For

Retailers, beverage producers and importers must adjust operations to comply with SDR including:

  • Displaying deposit fees separately on invoices and receipts
  • Updating ERP, POS, and accounting systems
  • Handling containers returns and storing them securely
  • Coordinating with SDR Portugal for reporting and reimbursement processes Failure to comply may result in penalties under environmental and waste‑management regulations.

Impact for SAP Customers (What Must Be Done)

To comply with SDR, SAP customers need to implement three key changes:

  • Create a Dedicated Pricing Condition
    • A specific SAP condition type must be added to represent the €0.10 SDR deposit per bottle.
    • The SDR deposit has no VAT
  • Adjust Document Layouts that must clearly show:
    • the deposit line,
    • the value €0.10 per unit,
    • and that this is a non‑taxable (no VAT) item.
  • Update SAF‑T Reporting
    • The SDR deposit must be correctly transmitted in the SAF‑T PT file, treated similarly to tax‑like material charges.
    • In the tax like material table, the relevant pricing condition must be classified as ‘E’ – Special consumer Taxes

Conclusion

SDR represents a transformative step for Portugal, modernizing the country’s recycling system, encouraging responsible consumption, and aligning national practice with European circular‑economy goals. For businesses, it introduces new compliance obligations but also strengthens sustainability credentials. For consumers, it offers a simple, rewarding way to contribute to a cleaner environment.

With the launch of VOLTA on 10 April 2026, Portugal joins 18 other European countries already leveraging deposit systems successfully—and takes a significant leap toward a more circular future.

 

Hope you enjoyed this article. Let me know your thoughts in the comments. 

Elsa

entry.title: Re: Pulling security logs from SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content: Hi! 🙂 What type of security logs do you specifically need? API activity, User login/authentication events, Compliance/audit logs or any other? This is important to understand better your request.

I have the same issue, were you able to solve this?

Thank you,

Luísa

entry.title: I want to Create a REST Data Entity using BTP Destination. but unable retrieve expected output.
entry.content:
Hi All,

I want to create a REST Data Entity from my build apps workspace for 'API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV' (/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/) odata service.

I have a BTP destination already configured.

1.Adding BTP Destination in Base section2. adding odata path in the retrieve section3.Test the data entity. got200

4.But not receiving the expected databut when I am retrieving data I am getting below result, it returns status -200. which is OK.

 

But I am not at all expecting this response. It is returning bunch of standard available catalog details. so something is wrong here.

 

Can someone please guide me what is going wrong here.

Is there any specific way I should configure my BTP Destination?

entry.title: Re: Update to next-gen Ariba Invoicing
entry.content: This link is very useful. Have any customers gone ahead with scenario 2, e.g. provisioning a fresh tenant whilst mid-implementation? This is our scenario & understandable is causing some concern around timelines, duplication of effort & potential risks associated with moving to something new. We have already faced numerous issues with technical setup of customer-managed CIM during build, so there is little desire to face these or equivalents again.

entry.title: Re: Getting started with the SAP Logistics Management setup
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a question regarding the implementation.

in our demosystem we don't have the Tab "Home" in our navigation interface

Can please give me some advice how to make it visible?

Thank in advance

Best regards

Santiago

https://me.sap.com/roadmapviewer/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapoverviewpage/82b2db84548d41209cda972f0fac428b

entry.title: Re: Week 2 : Use Joule Chat to create ABAP RAP Application and explore the Explain feature
entry.content: Week 2 Done!

entry.title: Storage of e-invoicing identifiers for SAP DRC implementation - EDOEUBUPAV vs Master Data
entry.content:
Hello 

We are now implementing SAP DRC and our SAP system is ERP 6.0. Our implementation partner tells us that to store e-invoicing identifiers we must use external (not linked with master data) table EDOEUBUPAV and using master data for this purpose is "reserved" for SAP HANA only. Can that be true? I can see in customizing section where I can assign Partner ID type to the country specific identifier and then manage the IDs exclusively in BP master data:

And then using new types for storing Peppol (and others depending on country) directly in BP master (Identification numbers section).

Would you please advise if this is really impossible for SAP ERP linked with DRC?

Thanks

Robert

entry.title: Re: Compare two different versions of the same crystal re...
entry.content:
You have to click on Export and then you'll see the types as Ido listed. Then use a Text file compare option to compare both. Microsoft has one: https://apps.microsoft.com/detail/9nt24mm809v5?hl=en-GB&gl=CA

For subreports you will need to save the subreports manually from CRD by drilling into it and then clicking on SaveAs

entry.title: Re: Provisioning of Business Data Cloud : SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: Is it possible to share the rights to edit license types with another S-user ID?

entry.title: SAP BTP Audit Log Access Just Got Easier - Here’s What’s New
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

🚀We’re excited to announce that the new Audit Log Viewer is officially live!

This release brings a faster, clearer, and more intuitive way to explore audit activity. Whether you’re tracking changes, investigating issues, or simply keeping an eye on system activity, the new viewer is designed to help you find what you need quickly and confidently.

🎯Key Changes 

1. No More Subaccount-Level Subscription

You no longer need to create a separate Audit Log Viewer subscription for each  subaccount. The viewer is now automatically available in every subaccount, with entitlement and subscription setup handled behind the scenes.

2. Global Account Viewer Now Included

Previously, there was no Audit Log Viewer for Global Accounts. With this update, a viewer is now available directly in the BTP cockpit, without creating a dedicated instance. Just open the cockpit and access it from the Global Account level.

The new changes can be referred from the link : What's new for SAP Business Technology Platform 

These changes  simplify operations, reduce configuration work, and make audit visibility consistent across your entire BTP landscape.

entry.title: Re: Mass close service calls via .NET SDK and B1UP
entry.content: Hi, I also want to do it with the 'Close' Button, but it ist not visible. How can I make ist visible, what conditions in the service call are needed for the button to appear?

entry.title: Re: ABAP Restful, annotation for navigaton with condition
entry.content: @csaba_szommer What annotation are you going to use for the external navigation? There are various kinds of annotations in the document. https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/d782acf8bfd74107ad6a04f0361c5f62

entry.title: Re: Installation of Crystal Reports 2025 damages installa...
entry.content:
I doubt SAP would do that, it would require some kind of logic to detect if CRD was installed  and your specific details etc....

The best way would be for you to use your install details and write your own installer to check possible scenerios.

You can use  CRD's silent installer, one CRD is installed then repair your CRforVS installer.

Located in FAQ doc: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/crystal-reports/faq

Here's how to use Silent Installer:

 
Do you want to do a silent installation of the software?

You can perform all the maintenance operations (Modify, Repair and Remove) remotely like it was already possible for Installation and Update by using the same command lines “-w” and “-r” along with the response file.

More details on how to do this are provided here: https://blogs.sap.com/2020/06/13/silent-installation-enhancements-introduced-in-bi-4.3/

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit – Migrate Data Directly from SAP System – Using Staging Tables
entry.content:
Hi ,

This most useful and Required  feature for every customer .  All S/4 Hana Version must have this feature. Please consider this request  at highest Priority .

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Audit Log Access Just Got Easier - Here’s What’s New
entry.content:
It was already, no?

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/audit-logs-made-simple-instant-access-to-the-sap-audit-log-viewer-in-sap/bc-p/14342845

 

And dkes it actually work with Cloud Identity Services now, or still only with S-users.

entry.title: Re: Guide to Korn Ferry and SuccessFactors Integration
entry.content: Any news on how the Korn Ferry integration works functionally?

entry.title: Re: ASP.NET Crystal Report Viewer failing to display a re...
entry.content:
You'll need to do more debugging.

Start by removing whole sections, save the report under a new name test again.

Once you find the source update your post with the info, unless you can find the solution...

Delete any unused formula or parameters that are not used in the report. SDK assumes the report works 100%.


at bobj.crv.LeftPanel.init (allInOne.js:34:68838)

suggests it may have something to do with the Parameter/Group display.

Verify the report in CRD, see if that finds anything.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Restful, annotation for navigaton with condition
entry.content: @MioYasutake I would like to use intent-based navigation (annotation semanticObject: and semanticObjectAction). The challange is how I can achieve it for two kinds of objects as I explained it in my original post.

entry.title: The Bill Note document number is not running according to the fiscal year.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I would like to ask about the document numbering.
I currently use the "Manage Payment Receipt – Thailand" app to print the Bill Note document for customers.

However, the External ID number is not reset after the new year.
The Manage Payment Receipt – Thailand app continues running the External ID number from the previous year instead of restarting for the new year.

Please advise if there is any configuration that allows the External ID to reset by fiscal year.

Best regards,

May

entry.content:
Hello,

I have a question regarding the implementation.

in our demosystem we don't have the Tab "Home" in our navigation interface

 

 

Can please give me some advice how to make it visible?

Thank in advance

Best regards

Santiago

Hi @SureshSubramanian ,

Can you try to use the workaround mentioned in SAP Note 3418948 to verify if it helps?

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Support

entry.title: Why supplier IBAN is not recognized in SAP Central Invoice Management with S/4 Hana Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Dear community, 

We have set up integration between SAP Central Invoice Management and S/4Hana Public Cloud.

After uploading the invoice, the Payment Method and IBAN (Partner Bank Type field) are empty and not recognized.

Is this functionality supported at all?

And if yes, what could be the reason for not recognizing the IBAN from the PDF file?

Thanks,

Kostadin
